agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Leave management module within an HR software system. Governs how employees accrue, request, consume, and carry over paid leave. The module must enforce accrual-based balance integrity, advance-notice policies, manager approval workflows, and year-end carryover limits.","bounded_context":"Leave Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Leave Balance","definition":"The current number of accrued but unused leave days available to an employee. Cannot be negative."},{"term":"Accrual","definition":"The process by which an employee earns 1.5 leave days per month worked, credited to their leave balance."},{"term":"Leave Request","definition":"A formal submission by an employee to use accrued leave days for a specified date range, subject to advance-notice rules and manager approval."},{"term":"Medical Leave","definition":"A category of leave that may be submitted retroactively (up to 5 calendar days after the absence) provided a doctor's note is attached."},{"term":"Working Days","definition":"Business days excluding weekends and public holidays, used to calculate the 3-working-day advance notice requirement."},{"term":"Doctor's Note","definition":"A medical certificate that must accompany any retroactively submitted medical leave request to make it valid."},{"term":"Carryover","definition":"The process at year-end where unused leave days transfer to the next calendar year, capped at 10 days. Days above 10 are forfeited."},{"term":"Contract End Date","definition":"The date on which an employee's employment contract terminates. Leave requests must not extend beyond this date."},{"term":"Forfeiture","definition":"The loss of accrued leave days that exceed the 10-day carryover cap at the end of a calendar year."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Employees accrue 1.5 days of leave per month worked.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The accrual rate is a policy parameter that HR leadership could adjust. The system must support an accrual rate, but 1.5 is a business policy, not a structural constraint.","testable_assertion":"After an employee completes N full months of service within a year, their total accrual for that year equals N × 1.5 days."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"An employee's leave balance must never go below zero. Leave cannot be taken before it is accrued.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A negative balance means the employee has consumed leave they never earned — this is a data integrity violation. The system state would be corrupt if the balance went negative.","testable_assertion":"For any employee at any point in time: leave_balance >= 0 AND approved_leave_days <= total_accrued_days - previously_consumed_days."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Non-medical leave requests must be submitted at least 3 working days before the leave start date.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 3-working-day notice period is an administrative policy that management could shorten or lengthen without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"Given a non-medical leave request submitted on date S with leave starting on date L, the number of working days between S (exclusive) and L (inclusive) must be >= 3, otherwise the request is rejected."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Medical leave can be submitted retroactively within 5 calendar days of the absence, provided a doctor's note is attached.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 5-day window and documentation requirement are policy decisions. The retroactive allowance is a business accommodation, not a structural constraint.","testable_assertion":"A medical leave request for absence date A submitted on date S is valid only if S - A <= 5 calendar days AND a doctor's note is attached. Submissions without a doctor's note or beyond 5 days are rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Only the employee's manager can approve or reject a leave request.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Approval authority is an organizational policy. The company could extend approval rights to HR or skip-level managers without corrupting system integrity.","testable_assertion":"A leave request transitions to 'approved' or 'rejected' only through an action by the employee's designated manager."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Rejected leave does not consume leave balance.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If rejected leave consumed balance, the system would be in an inconsistent state — days deducted with no corresponding approved absence. This is a state integrity rule.","testable_assertion":"When a leave request is rejected, the employee's leave balance remains unchanged from its value before the request was submitted."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"A leave request must not span across (extend beyond) the employee's contract end date.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Granting leave beyond a contract end date would authorize absence for a period when the employment relationship no longer exists. This is a logical consistency constraint.","testable_assertion":"For any leave request, the last day of requested leave must be <= the employee's contract end date. Requests violating this are rejected at submission."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"At the end of each calendar year, unused leave above 10 days is forfeited. A maximum of 10 days carries over to the next year.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 10-day carryover cap is a policy threshold that could be adjusted by HR leadership. The forfeiture mechanism itself is policy, not a structural constraint.","testable_assertion":"On January 1 of each year, if an employee's leave balance as of December 31 exceeds 10 days, their new balance is set to exactly 10 days. If balance <= 10, it carries over in full."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An employee has worked for 4 full months and taken no leave","when":"The system calculates their leave balance","then":"The balance is 6.0 days (4 × 1.5)"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 2 days","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request for 3 days","then":"The request is rejected because the requested days exceed the available balance"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An employee submits a non-medical leave request on a Monday","when":"The requested leave starts on the following Wednesday (2 working days later)","then":"The request is rejected for insufficient advance notice (requires 3 working days)"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An employee submits a non-medical leave request on a Monday","when":"The requested leave starts on the following Thursday (3 working days later)","then":"The request passes the advance-notice validation"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An employee was absent due to illness 4 calendar days ago","when":"They submit a medical leave request with a valid doctor's note","then":"The request is accepted for processing (within the 5-day retroactive window)"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An employee was absent due to illness 6 calendar days ago","when":"They submit a medical leave request with a doctor's note","then":"The request is rejected because it exceeds the 5-day retroactive submission window"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An employee submits a medical leave request retroactively within 5 days","when":"No doctor's note is attached","then":"The request is rejected for missing required documentation"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A manager rejects a pending leave request for 3 days","when":"The rejection is processed","then":"The employee's leave balance remains unchanged — no days are deducted"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"An employee's contract ends on March 15","when":"They submit a leave request for March 13–17","then":"The request is rejected because it spans beyond the contract end date"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An employee has 14 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The year-end carryover process runs","then":"Their balance on January 1 is set to 10 days and 4 days are forfeited"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An employee has 8 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The year-end carryover process runs","then":"Their balance on January 1 is 8 days (no forfeiture, under cap)"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A manager approves a 2-day leave request for an employee with 5 days balance","when":"The approval is processed","then":"The employee's leave balance is reduced to 3 days"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Employee submits leave request on the same day leave is accrued","expected_behavior":"The accrual should be credited before the balance check, so the newly accrued days are available for the request."},{"scenario":"Leave request starts on the day after the contract end date","expected_behavior":"Rejected — the leave period is entirely outside the employment contract."},{"scenario":"Employee has exactly 10 days unused at year-end","expected_behavior":"All 10 days carry over; no forfeiture occurs (the cap is 'above 10 days')."},{"scenario":"Medical leave request submitted on exactly the 5th calendar day after absence","expected_behavior":"Accepted — the 5-day window is inclusive ('within 5 days')."},{"scenario":"Leave request spans a period that includes public holidays or weekends","expected_behavior":"The system must clarify whether non-working days within a leave span consume leave balance or not. This is not specified in the current rules."},{"scenario":"Employee's contract end date falls mid-way through a requested leave period","expected_behavior":"Rejected — the request spans beyond the contract end date, even if it starts before it."},{"scenario":"Employee submits a non-medical leave request exactly 3 working days in advance but a public holiday falls within those 3 days","expected_behavior":"The 3-day rule counts working days only, so the public holiday does not count. The submission may fail the advance notice check depending on when the holiday falls."},{"scenario":"Partial month worked (e.g., employee joins mid-month)","expected_behavior":"The spec says '1.5 days per month worked' — unclear whether partial months accrue pro-rata or require full months. This needs clarification."},{"scenario":"Year-end carryover with pending (unapproved) leave requests","expected_behavior":"Unclear whether pending requests should be considered as consumed when calculating carryover balance. Safest approach: carryover is based on actual approved consumption only."},{"scenario":"Manager attempts to approve a request after the employee's balance has been reduced by another approval","expected_behavior":"Balance must be re-validated at approval time — not just at submission time — to prevent over-consumption due to concurrent approvals."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect accrual or carryover logic could result in employees taking more leave than earned, directly impacting labor costs and productivity. Forfeiture errors could create financial liability if legally challenged.","operational":"Advance-notice rules ensure adequate staffing coverage. Medical leave retroactivity accommodates genuine illness while the 5-day cap prevents abuse. Year-end carryover caps prevent excessive leave accumulation that could disrupt future operations.","user":"Employees need a transparent, predictable leave system. Incorrect balance calculations or opaque rejection reasons erode trust. The medical leave retroactive window provides essential flexibility for genuine health situations.","risk":"Primary risk is balance integrity — if negative balances are possible, the system is fundamentally broken. Secondary risk is carryover miscalculation creating legal exposure. Concurrent approval race conditions could silently violate the non-negative balance invariant.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Employees","impact":"Directly affected by accrual accuracy, balance transparency, request submission rules, and carryover outcomes. Errors reduce trust and satisfaction."},{"group":"Managers","impact":"Responsible for approval workflow. Need clear visibility into team leave schedules and employee balances to make informed decisions."},{"group":"HR / Payroll","impact":"Depends on accurate leave data for payroll processing, compliance reporting, and policy enforcement. Carryover and forfeiture directly affect year-end reconciliation."},{"group":"Finance","impact":"Leave accruals represent a financial liability on the balance sheet. Incorrect balances affect financial reporting accuracy."}]},"confidence":{"level":82,"reasoning":"The specification is well-defined with clear, unambiguous rules covering accrual, balance constraints, advance notice, medical leave exceptions, approval workflow, contract boundaries, and carryover limits. Confidence is not higher because a few operational details are unspecified (partial month accrual, weekend/holiday counting within leave spans, concurrency handling at approval time, treatment of pending requests at year-end).","high_confidence_areas":["Non-negative balance invariant","Accrual rate and mechanism","Advance notice requirement for non-medical leave","Medical leave retroactive submission rules","Rejected leave does not consume balance","Contract end date boundary","Carryover cap and forfeiture"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether partial months accrue pro-rata or require a full month","Whether weekends and public holidays within a leave span consume leave days","How pending/unapproved requests are treated during year-end carryover","Race condition handling when multiple requests are approved concurrently against the same balance","Definition of 'working days' — which holiday calendar applies"],"assumptions":["The 5-day retroactive window for medical leave is measured in calendar days, not working days","'Month worked' means a full calendar month of employment for accrual purposes","Leave balance is checked both at submission and at approval time","The carryover process runs once at the boundary of December 31 to January 1","The 3-working-day notice is measured from submission date (exclusive) to leave start date (inclusive)"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
